systems.name: Tritone Substitution Soloing Framework
systems.summary: This is the book's single central system. It operates over the II V I progression by substituting the V7 chord with a dominant 7th chord whose root lies a tritone away (subV7), then prescribing four reusable soloing options — dom7 arpeggio, dom9 arpeggio, major pentatonic, and Mixolydian mode — each applied over the substitute chord. The system's defining move is tone-mapping: every scale degree of each soloing option is analytically traced back to its altered-extension function (b9, #11, b13) on the original V7. Avoidance and approach-note rules govern specific tones within each option to ensure idiomatic phrasing, and chromaticism is sanctioned as a voice-leading device to connect the substituted bar to the tonic resolution. The entire framework is explicitly designed to be transposed intact to any key, making transposition a first-class traversal rule rather than an afterthought.

members.name: ii chord (minor 7)
members.summary: The Dm7 chord occupying bar 1 of the canonical II V I; soloed over with Dorian, Dorian bebop, or a matching arpeggio.

members.name: Original V7 chord (G7)
members.summary: The unsubstituted dominant 7th in bar 2; serves as the harmonic reference against which all altered-tone labels (b9, #11, b13) are calculated.

members.name: Substitute V7 / subV7 (Db7)
members.summary: The tritone-substituted dominant, root a tritone away from G7; the chord the guitarist actually solos over using the four prescribed options.

members.name: I chord (CM7)
members.summary: The tonic major-7 resolution chord in bar 3; target of chromatic voice-leading approach from the subV7 bar.

members.name: Soloing Option 1 — dom7 Arpeggio
members.summary: 1-3-5-b7 arpeggio built on the subV7 root (Db7); each tone mapped to an altered extension of the original G7.

members.name: Soloing Option 2 — dom9 Arpeggio
members.summary: 1-3-5-b7-9 arpeggio; extends option 1 by adding the ninth, introducing the b13 color against G7.

members.name: Soloing Option 3 — Major Pentatonic Scale
members.summary: 1-2-3-5-6 pentatonic built on the subV7 root; provides #11, b13, b9 colors against G7 with the sixth treated as an approach note only.

members.name: Soloing Option 4 — Mixolydian Mode
members.summary: 1-2-3-4-5-6-b7 mode built on the subV7 root; a superset of the pentatonic adding the 4th (11) and b7; specific degrees (Gb, B) governed by avoidance rules.

members.name: ii-chord Option — Dorian Bebop Scale
members.summary: Dorian mode with an added chromatic passing tone (F#); used to solo over the ii chord (Dm7), extending the harmonic vocabulary beyond the dominant substitution.

traversal_rules.name: Measure-Slot Assignment
traversal_rules.summary: Each bar of the three-bar II V I receives a specific soloing-option assignment (e.g., Dm7 arpeggio in bar 1, descending Db7 arpeggio in bar 2, CM7 arpeggio in bar 3), governing how the player moves through the progression chord-by-chord.

references.quote_excerpt: Bar 1: Dm7 arpeggio Bar 2: Descending Db7 arpeggio Bar 3: CM7 arpeggio

traversal_rules.name: Key-Transposition Portability
traversal_rules.summary: The entire substitution framework — substitution rule, four options, tone mappings, avoidance rules — is explicitly designed to be transposed intact to any key, making key-shift a primary traversal operation rather than a secondary adaptation.

references.topic: Tritone sub over II V I
references.quote_excerpt: play scales and arpeggios starting from the b5 of the V7 chord

references.topic: II V I tritone sub example
references.quote_excerpt: The idea behind is to play Db over G7

traversal_rules.name: Chromatic Resolution Approach
traversal_rules.summary: Chromaticism is prescribed as the preferred voice-leading device to connect the subV7 soloing bar to the tonic CM7 chord, giving the resolution a smooth stepwise arrival.

references.quote_excerpt: chromaticism to approach the root of CM7

modification_rules.name: Tritone Substitution — Replace V7 with subV7
modification_rules.summary: The core substitution rule: any V7 chord in a II V I may be replaced by a dom7 chord whose root lies a tritone (b5) away, enabling the four prescribed soloing options to generate altered-extension colors against the original dominant.

references.quote_excerpt: play another 7th chord that has its root a tritone away from the original

references.topic: II V I tritone sub example
references.quote_excerpt: After applying a tritone substitution to the V7 chord we get Dm7 | Db7 | CM7

modification_rules.name: Altered-Tone Color Mapping
modification_rules.summary: Every scale degree of each soloing option over the subV7 must be analytically labelled as its altered-extension function on the original V7 (b9, #11, b13, or chord tone), making color-tone tracing a required step before any option is deployed in a lick.

references.quote_excerpt: The root of Db7 highlights the #11/b5 of G7
Db is the #11 of the G7 chord. Eb is the b13 of G7. Ab is the b9 of G7
Db is the #11 of the G7 chord. Ab is the b9 of G7. Eb is the b13 of G7

modification_rules.name: Approach-Note Avoidance Rule
modification_rules.summary: Specific scale degrees within a soloing option that do not map cleanly to altered extensions or chord tones of the original V7 must be treated as approach notes — used in passing to target a chord tone — and must never be sustained as resting tones.

engine_payload.treatment: approach note only, not a resting tone

references.quote_excerpt: The sixth (Bb) of Db7 should be used as an approach note in order to target a chord tone
The seventh of Db7 (B) is the third of G7

modification_rules.name: Dorian Bebop Chromatic Passing Tone Addition
modification_rules.summary: The Dorian mode over the ii chord is modified by inserting a chromatic passing tone (F# between F and G in D Dorian) to produce the Dorian bebop scale, smoothing voice-leading across the bar and connecting more fluidly to the subV7.

engine_payload.added_tone: chromatic passing tone (F# in D Dorian)

references.quote_excerpt: D Dorian bebop (D-E-F-F#-G-A-B-C). The Dorian bebop scale is the Dorian mode with a passing tone

modification_rules.name: dom7 to dom9 Arpeggio Extension
modification_rules.summary: The dom7 arpeggio option (option 1) may be extended to the dom9 arpeggio (option 2) by adding the ninth, introducing the b13 color against the original V7 — a tone-set expansion of a soloing option, not a voicing-density change.

references.quote_excerpt: add the ninth to the previous dom7 arpeggio
The root of Db7 highlights the #11/b5 of G7
